workflows.name: Visual Enhancement Utility v1.2.1
workflows.notes: v1.2.1 adds Sprint 38 authoritative visual_affordances[] consumption (legacy/hybrid/authoritative modes); v1.2 hook topology unchanged.
workflows.artefacts: Enhanced HTML package with pedagogically placed visuals

workflowOutputSpec.goal: Generate an enhanced learner-page HTML package by selecting pedagogically significant visuals from PRISM visual affordance hooks.
workflowOutputSpec.constraints: traditional_html_image_refs: true; downloadable_html_required: true; manifest_derived_from_inserted_figures_only: true; no_base64: true; no_inline_svg: true; one_image_per_step2_run: true; use_prism_visual_affordance_hooks: true; hooks_are_candidates_not_obligations: true; no_visual_preemption_of_tasks: true

steps.title: Create Enhanced HTML Package
steps.roleLabel: Hook-based HTML enhancement and manifest generation
steps.override_prompt_body:
TASK Analyse the uploaded HTML file and create an enhanced HTML package with pedagogically useful figures. IMPORTANT ARCHITECTURE PRISM owns safe visual placement opportunities. The page may contain hidden hooks such as:
Treat these hooks as the candidate opportunity set. Hooks may include data-affordance-id, data-activity-id, and data-visual-activity-id for Sprint 38 matching. SPRINT 38 VISUAL AFFORDANCE HANDOVER (optional second input) Authors may attach page compose JSON (or visual_affordances extract) alongside the HTML file. The JSON may include: visual_affordance_schema_version, activities_visual_review[], visual_affordances[]. Detect and report handover_mode in Step 1 JSON response: legacy | hybrid | authoritative. HANDOVER MODES LEGACY — visual_affordances[] missing or empty: - Apply all v1.2 rules below unchanged (PEDAGOGICAL SIGNIFICANCE, hook inference, topic avoidance heuristics). - Do not require handover JSON. HYBRID — visual_affordances[] present but incomplete handover: - For each hook, if a matching affordance record exists (same activity_id + visual_slot, or data-affordance-id match), JSON controls pedagogy. - Unmatched hooks: legacy inference only if activities_visual_review for that activity_id does not have activity_visual_value.decision: none. - Never override visual_decision: reject or defer with inference-generated figures. AUTHORITATIVE — full Sprint 38 handover (visual_affordance_schema_version 38.4, activities_visual_review[], visual_affordances[]): - HTML locates slots only; JSON controls generate / defer / reject. - Do not create additional opportunities from data-visual-subject, headings, or activity title alone. - Do not promote visual_decision: reject to generate. Do not promote defer to generate without explicit author override in selection_notes. HOOK MATCHING (when JSON present) Match .util-visual-affordance hooks using: - data-visual-slot (must equal affordance visual_slot for generate) - data-visual-activity-id or data-activity-id (must equal affordance activity_id) - data-affordance-id when present (must equal affordance affordance_id) VISUAL_DECISION: generate Insert figure and queue image only when a generate record matches the hook. Do not infer purpose, representation, claim boundaries, or tier when the generate record supplies them — consume: purpose, preferred_representation, pedagogical_added_value, reasoning_supported, representation_avoid, allowed_claims, disallowed_claims, must_show, must_not_show, source_basis, requires_exact_data_match, anti_spoiler, spoiler_boundary, learner_stage, canonical_discipline_note, caption_intent, discipline_risk_level, tier, affordance_id, visual_slot. Build each image_queue prompt from those fields (not "illustrate the topic"). Include representation_avoid and disallowed_claims as hard negatives in the image prompt. PEDAGOGICAL ADDED VALUE (Sprint 38-6 — when present on generate records) When pedagogical_added_value is supplied, treat it as the primary statement of what new cognitive support the image must provide beyond surrounding materials. Generate the pedagogical support, not merely the visual structure matching preferred_representation. Reject queueing when the brief would only duplicate a blank worksheet, duplicate a table, duplicate answer structure, topic poster, illustrated summary, or generic infographic shell. For classification_matrix: image must add discriminating cues and category distinctions — not an empty grid mirroring the learner worksheet. For comparison_framework: add comparison dimensions and confusable features — not decorative columns duplicating the table. For causal_model: show mechanism visibility and directionality — not isolated topic labels. For evidence_t_chart: add evaluation criteria scaffolding — not a copied evidence list. For number_line_segments: depict exact source endpoints only — never invented intervals. Use must_show / must_not_show / reasoning_supported / pedagogical_added_value together; preferred_representation names layout family only. QUANTITATIVE FIDELITY — when requires_exact_data_match is true: - No invented values, trends, interval endpoints, or unsupported quantitative relationships. - Numbers and labels in image prompt and figure may come only from source_basis and allowed_claims. VISUAL_DECISION: defer - No figure, no image_queue entry for that affordance_id. - Copy affordance_id, defer_reason, rationale to deferred_affordances[] in Step 1 JSON. VISUAL_DECISION: reject - No figure, no image_queue entry; do not invent a substitute opportunity for the same slot. - Copy affordance_id, rejection_reason, rationale to rejected_affordances[] in Step 1 JSON. - Never reclassify reject to generate. ACCESSIBILITY (generate rows) - figcaption: use caption_intent (visible text). - alt: same instructional support as caption_intent for non-sighted learners; respect must_not_show and spoiler_boundary (hide answers, classification keys, model solution when flagged). - Do not use alt_text_intent; do not state numbers in alt unless requires_exact_data_match is false OR numbers are in source_basis. FIGURE STRUCTURE (generate) Use data-affordance-id on figure when affordance_id is known: Do NOT independently invent new placement locations by heading proximity or general page scanning unless there are no hooks at all. If no hooks exist, use conservative placement and say so in the JSON response. OUTPUT REQUIREMENTS Create a downloadable HTML file named: enhanced-learner-page.html Then return a compact image generation queue. Do not paste the HTML into chat. HTML REQUIREMENTS - Preserve the original HTML structure and content as far as possible. - Insert only pedagogically useful figures. - Use normal HTML image references: - Use PNG filenames beginning with VO ids. - Do not use base64. - Do not use inline SVG. - Generated images must be responsive and must not overflow the page or viewport. - Leave unused .util-visual-affordance hooks in place unless removing them is necessary for clean output. PLACEMENT CONTRACT Use PRISM hooks as candidate slots. Hooks are candidates, not obligations. For each hook: - inspect its data-visual-slot, data-visual-subject, and data-visual-activity-id - inspect nearby activity/material content - decide whether a figure would materially improve learning - insert a figure only when the pedagogical case is strong Do not add a figure merely because a hook exists. PEDAGOGICAL SIGNIFICANCE RULES Insert visuals where they materially improve: - reasoning - interpretation - comparison - sequencing - mechanism understanding - spatial/system relationships - misconception repair - transfer to related cases Prefer high-value visual roles: - causal mechanism diagrams - comparison structures - process or sequence models - system maps - classification frameworks - evidence-to-interpretation models - misconception contrast diagrams - transfer/limit diagrams Avoid low-value visuals: - decorative illustrations - generic topic images - visual restatements of headings - diagrams that merely repeat nearby prose - diagrams that duplicate an existing table without adding interpretation - one figure per activity by default There is no fixed image count. Many visuals are acceptable when each has strong pedagogical significance. Few or none are acceptable when figures would add little. INCREMENTAL VALUE RULE Each inserted figure must add something the surrounding text/materials do not already provide clearly. Reject a candidate when the figure would: - restate a simple definition - duplicate a table - repeat a list visually - add visual noise without helping the learner reason - overlap substantially with another selected figure ANTI-SPOILER / ANTI-PREEMPTION RULES Before inserting a figure, identify the intellectual work the learner is being asked to do. Do not insert visuals that reveal, complete, classify, order, solve, or evaluate the core learner task before the learner attempts it. Avoid pre-task visuals that give away: - the correct order in a sequencing activity - the correct categories in a classification activity - the completed causal chain in a construction activity - the correct matching pairs in a matching activity - the conclusion in an evaluation/judgement task - the corrected form in an error-spotting task - the interpretation in a graph/data interpretation activity A visual should support the reasoning process, not replace it. If a complete model answer would be useful, place it only after the relevant learner attempt and label it as a comparison/checking figure where appropriate. SAFE PRE-TASK VISUALS Before a task, prefer visuals that: - orient without solving - show an abstract framework rather than the specific answer - show the kind of relation learners will reason about - clarify terms needed to attempt the task - provide a partial scaffold without completing the work FIGURE STRUCTURE For every inserted figure: - create exactly one image queue entry - ensure the HTML reference and queue entry match exactly Use this figure structure: RESPONSIVE FIGURE CSS Add this CSS once if equivalent rules are not already present: .util-figure--pedagogic{ margin:1.25rem 0; padding:1rem; border:1px solid #e5e7eb; border-radius:1rem; background:#fff; box-shadow:0 1px 3px rgba(0,0,0,.08); max-width:100%; overflow:hidden; } .util-figure--pedagogic img{ display:block; width:100%; max-width:100%; height:auto; object-fit:contain; border-radius:.75rem; } .util-figure--pedagogic figcaption{ margin-top:.75rem; font-size:.95rem; color:#374151; } @media (max-width:720px){ .util-figure--pedagogic{padding:.75rem} } IMAGE QUEUE FORMAT Each queue entry must contain: - id - filename - prompt - affordance_id (when from Sprint 38 generate) - requires_exact_data_match (boolean when applicable) The prompt must describe a polished educational PNG image and must not request SVG. Return concise JSON only: { "handover_mode": "legacy | hybrid | authoritative", "html_file": "enhanced-learner-page.html", "file_status": "downloadable file created", "hooks_found": 0, "figures_inserted": 0, "images_required": 0, "rejected_affordances": [ { "affordance_id": "...", "rejection_reason": "...", "rationale": "..." } ], "deferred_affordances": [ { "affordance_id": "...", "defer_reason": "...", "rationale": "..." } ], "image_queue": [ { "id": "VO1", "affordance_id": "va-...", "filename": "VO1-example.png", "prompt": "Structured brief from Sprint 38 generate fields...", "requires_exact_data_match": false } ], "rename_checklist": [ "VO1-example.png" ], "selection_notes": [ "Per figure: affordance_id, purpose, and why it does not pre-empt the learner task." ] } FAILURE HANDLING If the downloadable file link is missing or unavailable, return: { "file_status": "blocked_no_downloadable_file", "what_to_do": "Ask the chat to create a downloadable link for the generated html file." } VISUAL STYLE GUIDANCE Prefer polished instructional diagrams over abstract placeholder frameworks. Educational figures should: - use clear semantic colour coding - label important transitions and relationships - show movement or change explicitly with arrows where appropriate - include equilibrium or comparison markers when pedagogically useful - use clean textbook-style composition - combine diagram plus concise explanatory framing - feel suitable for a university teaching handout or textbook Prefer: - publication-quality educational graphics - explanatory composition - canonical disciplinary visual conventions - diagrams that clarify difficult relationships Avoid: - skeletal placeholder diagrams - unlabeled conceptual shells - decorative infographic clutter - stock-art aesthetics - excessive text density - presentation-slide clip-art style visuals DISCIPLINE CONVENTIONS Where a discipline has canonical diagram conventions, follow them accurately. Examples: - Economics: AD/AS diagrams should show correct demand and supply shifts - Science: process diagrams should preserve correct causal flow - Statistics: graphs should use appropriate axes and scales - Geography: maps and spatial diagrams should preserve meaningful relationships Visual accuracy matters as much as visual polish.
steps.notes:
1. Upload PRISM HTML + optional visual_affordances JSON.
2. Run Step 1 (Sprint 38 authoritative/hybrid/legacy modes).
3. Save enhanced-learner-page.html and images/ folder.
4. Run Step 2 per queue item.

steps.title: Generate One Image
steps.override_prompt_body:
TASK

SPRINT 38 QUEUE CONSUMPTION

When the Step 1 queue entry includes affordance_id, requires_exact_data_match, or an expanded prompt from Sprint 38 fields:
- Treat the queue entry prompt as authoritative.
- Honour representation_avoid and disallowed_claims as hard negatives.
- Do not depict must_not_show items.
- When requires_exact_data_match is true, do not add numbers, trends, or interval boundaries not stated in the prompt.
- Honour spoiler_boundary omissions in the image.
- Do not illustrate activity title or topic alone.


Generate the next image from the Step 1 image queue.

IMAGE REQUIREMENTS

- Generate one image only.
- Create a polished educational PNG image.
- Use the next queue entry that has not yet been generated in this chat.
- Use the queue entry prompt and filename.
- Do not use SVG.
- Do not output base64.
- Do not include extra images.

OUTPUT FORMAT

Return concise JSON only:

{
  "generated_image": "VO1",
  "filename_to_use": "VO1-example.png",
  "rename_instruction": "Rename the downloaded image exactly to: VO1-example.png"
}

COMPLETION RULE

If all queued images have already been generated, say so clearly and stop.
steps.notes:
1. Copy the Step 2 prompt and run it.
2. Download the generated image.
3. Rename it exactly to the filename shown.
4. Place it inside the images folder.
5. Repeat until all queued images are generated.
